title: Sequential Bayes: Walking the Corridor

runtime.expected_outcome: Across 4+ steps the belief collapses from broad to near-point-mass as each new obs multiplicatively refines the posterior.

math.latex: P(s_t \mid o_{1:t}) \propto P(o_t \mid s_t) \sum_{s_{t-1}} P(s_t \mid s_{t-1}, a_{t-1}) \, P(s_{t-1} \mid o_{1:t-1})

audiences.kid: Every time you see another clue you shrink the list of possible rooms you could be in. After enough clues, you know exactly where you are.
audiences.real: Bayesian filtering: each observation multiplicatively refines the belief through the transition model. The 5x5 corridor makes the collapse visible.
audiences.equation: Forward filter: predict with B, then update with A. Posterior P(s_t | o_{1:t}) concentrates as log-likelihoods accumulate.
audiences.derivation: Starting from P(s_t, o_{1:t}) = P(o_t|s_t) sum_{s_{t-1}} P(s_t|s_{t-1}, a_{t-1}) P(s_{t-1}, o_{1:t-1}), the filtering recursion follows by marginalisation. This is eq 4.13 of Ch 4.

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate that the posterior sharpens monotonically across observations in a small corridor world.
orchestrate.role: Filtering tutor grounded in the POMDP bundle's perceive-plan-act loop.
orchestrate.context: A 5x5 corridor-with-turns maze where the agent walks from start to goal, re-sweeping its belief at every step.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
